title: Device Compliance Status Overview

compliance.assurance_rationale: Monitors device configuration compliance status against Catalyst Center policies, providing evidence of configuration baseline enforcement.
compliance.controlObjective: Provides continuous visibility into how many devices meet Catalyst Center compliance policies versus how many are out of policy for NIST 800-53 CM-6 (configuration settings).
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search `UC-5.13.28` over `index=catalyst` and `cisco:dnac:compliance` (scheduled daily), exported to the compliance evidence dashboard; alert optional when NON_COMPLIANT share exceeds the agreed threshold.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Demonstrates network device configuration compliance monitoring as part of firewall and router configuration standards.
compliance.controlObjective: Contributes to PCI DSS 1.1.1 by showing network security control configuration is monitored against an enforced baseline in Catalyst Center.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Same saved search and dashboard as the primary UC artefact, with a monthly PDF or CSV compliance snapshot for the CDE/network boundary device population covered by the feed.

dataSources: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:compliance`. from the `compliance` modular input in the Cisco Catalyst Add-on (Splunkbase 7538) polling `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/compliance`. Default poll interval: 3600s (1h). Volume: ~1 event per device per poll. RBAC: NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE. Key fields: `complianceStatus` (string), `complianceType` (string), `deviceName` (string), `deviceUuid` (string), `managementIpAddress` (string), `lastUpdateTime` (epoch milliseconds). CIM data model: Change.
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:compliance" | stats count by complianceStatus | eventstats sum(count) as _total | eval pct=round(100*count/_total,1)
description: Provides an overview of device compliance status across the managed infrastructure as reported by Catalyst Center compliance policies.
value: Compliance is a continuous requirement. This overview shows at a glance how many devices are compliant, non-compliant, or in error state.
implementation: Enable the `compliance` input in the Cisco Catalyst TA. The TA polls `GET /dna/intent/api/v2/compliance/detail` on a **900s** default interval. Key fields: `complianceStatus` (COMPLIANT, NON_COMPLIANT, ERROR, IN_PROGRESS, NOT_APPLICABLE), `deviceName`, `complianceType` (RUNNING_CONFIG, IMAGE, PSIRT, EOX, NETWORK_SETTINGS).
visualization: Pie or donut (count by complianceStatus), single value (compliant %), table with status and percentage.

references.title: Catalyst Center Compliance API ‚Äî Cisco DevNet

knownFalsePositives:
**Active configuration push marking devices temporarily non-compliant.** During a Catalyst Center template deployment, devices receive new configurations in stages. Between the push and the next compliance check, devices may show as NON_COMPLIANT because the running config does not yet match the golden template. Distinguish by correlating with `index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:audit:logs"` for recent template deployment activity. Suppress by excluding `complianceStatus=IN_PROGRESS` from non-compliance counts and allowing 2 poll cycles after a configuration push before alerting.

**Lab or pilot devices intentionally deviating from the golden template.** Lab switches or proof-of-concept equipment may run non-standard configurations that fail compliance checks by design. Distinguish by checking whether `deviceName` matches a lab naming convention. Suppress by maintaining a `catalyst_compliance_exceptions` lookup with `deviceName` and justification, and filtering excepted devices from compliance alerting.

**Compliance check returning ERROR due to device unreachability.** If Catalyst Center cannot reach a device to check its configuration, the compliance status may show ERROR rather than COMPLIANT or NON_COMPLIANT. Distinguish by correlating with `index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:devicehealth"` for `reachabilityHealth=Unreachable` on the same device. Suppress by treating ERROR as a separate condition from NON_COMPLIANT ‚Äî alert on reachability, not compliance.

**NOT_APPLICABLE compliance status for devices that do not support the compliance type.** Some compliance types (e.g., IMAGE compliance for APs, EOX for virtual appliances) return NOT_APPLICABLE for unsupported device families. Distinguish by checking whether `complianceType` is appropriate for the device's platform. Suppress by filtering `| where complianceStatus NOT IN ("NOT_APPLICABLE","IN_PROGRESS")` for compliance violation dashboards.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7538) installed; **compliance** feed landing in `index=catalyst` with sourcetype `cisco:dnac:compliance`.
‚Ä¢ Catalyst Center **2.2+** (confirm **Intent API v2** in your build); service account with **`NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE`** or higher read access to **Compliance** APIs (exact role names may vary by version‚Äîverify in ISE/AAA for the API user).
‚Ä¢ Retention and export policy aligned to **NIST/PCI** evidence needs (often **90‚Äì365 days** in the `catalyst` index or a **summary** index for compliance snapshots).
‚Ä¢ See `docs/implementation-guide.md` for `inputs.conf`, secure credential storage, and index sizing.

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
‚Ä¢ **Intent API:** `GET /dna/intent/api/v2/compliance/detail`.
‚Ä¢ **TA input name:** **compliance**; sourcetype `cisco:dnac:compliance`.
‚Ä¢ **Default interval:** **900 seconds (15 minutes)**‚Äîbalance API load vs freshness; shorten only if your change-approval process requires it.
‚Ä¢ **Key fields:** `complianceStatus` (**COMPLIANT**, **NON_COMPLIANT**, **ERROR**, **IN_PROGRESS**, **NOT_APPLICABLE**), `deviceName`, `complianceType` (**RUNNING_CONFIG**, **IMAGE**, **PSIRT**, **EOX**, **NETWORK_SETTINGS**).
‚Ä¢ **Volume note:** the TA may emit **one or many events** per poll‚Äîuse **dedup**/`latest` in downstream panels if you see duplicate **deviceName** + **complianceType** rows per bucket.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and report
```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:compliance" | stats count by complianceStatus | eventstats sum(count) as _total | eval pct=round(100*count/_total,1)
```

Understanding this SPL
‚Ä¢ **`stats` + `eventstats`** yields a **stable percentage** of each `complianceStatus` value for the selected window (prefer **`eventstats`** over `sum(count)` in **`eval`** alone for clarity at scale).
‚Ä¢ Filter with `complianceType=<value>` in a **post-process** or **base + append** if you need **separate** pies per policy family (for example **IMAGE** vs **RUNNING_CONFIG**).

**Pipeline walkthrough**
‚Ä¢ Scopes **`cisco:dnac:compliance`**; rolls up **counts** and attaches **%** of total for dashboard tiles and audit tables.

Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
‚Ä¢ **Compare the percentage of devices in each status (especially COMPLIANT vs NON_COMPLIANT) against the Catalyst Center Compliance dashboard** for the same scope and time‚ÄîSplunk and the UI should agree within one **poll**.
‚Ä¢ Spot-check a few **deviceName** values that appear as **ERROR** or **IN_PROGRESS** in Splunk and confirm the same in the GUI.
‚Ä¢ Run **`| timechart count by complianceStatus`** to ensure **continuous** ingestion; long gaps indicate TA or API issues, not a silent ‚Äúall green‚Äù network.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
‚Ä¢ **Dashboard:** donut or bar by **complianceStatus**, **single value** for **% COMPLIANT**, plus a **table** of top exceptions when filtered to **NON_COMPLIANT**.
‚Ä¢ **Evidence:** schedule this saved search **daily** and archive CSV/PDF to your GRC or evidence store per your control framework.
‚Ä¢ **IN_PROGRESS spikes** often follow **bulk template pushes**‚Äîannotate change windows on the panel so teams do not misread a temporary state.

Step 5 ‚Äî Troubleshooting
‚Ä¢ **No events:** enable **compliance** input, re-check **Catalyst** base URL, token, and role; search **`splunkd.log`** for **HTTP 401/403/429**.
‚Ä¢ **Partial statuses only:** your policies may be **unassigned** for some platforms‚Äî**NOT_APPLICABLE** dominance is a **Catalyst** configuration topic, not a Splunk parse bug.
‚Ä¢ **Count mismatch vs UI:** align **time zone**, **device inventory scope** (virtual domain / site), and **dedup** logic with what the operator filters in the **Compliance** UI.
‚Ä¢ **Stuck IN_PROGRESS:** confirm **on-device** or **Catalyst** jobs are not **stalled**; open **TAC** if compliance jobs **never** complete for a class of devices.
‚Ä¢ **No compliance events arriving:** verify the `compliance` input is enabled and check `index=_internal` for API error or endpoint timeout messages from the TA.
‚Ä¢ **NULL values in compliance status:** devices in the middle of a compliance check show IN_PROGRESS; empty values indicate the compliance engine has not run for that device.
‚Ä¢ **Fewer devices than expected:** check the service account role; credential access denied errors indicate the account lacks NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE.
grandmaExplanation: We show you how many of your network devices meet the required configuration standards and how many do not.
